dd48de7dc9048583.root_cause: OpenShift 4.12 bare-metal cluster installation failed during the telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup pre-phase. The ansible playbook on lab host 10.8.34.218 deployed cluster ptpcimno via kcli but openshift-installer never completed: /root/cluster_ready.txt was never created after 150 retries (~2h38m). Cluster
dd48de7dc9048583.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** OpenShift 4.12 bare-metal cluster installation failed during the telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup pre-phase. The ansible playbook on lab host 10.8.34.218 deployed cluster ptpcimno via kcli but openshift-installer never completed: /root/cluster_ready.txt was never created after 150 retries (~2h38m). ClusterVersion 4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-032308 remained Progressing with MultipleErrors for 144+ minutes — all 28+ cluster operators unavailable, oc get nodes returned no resources, and the API server at 10.8.34.117:6443 eventually became unreachable. PTP e2e tests never ran.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not identifiable in openshift/ptp-operator. Failure is in openshift-installer/cluster provisioning on the telco bare-metal lab (openshift/release CI), not PTP operator code. Prior successful runs on the same job used payload 4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-04-15-130410; this run used 4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-032308.

**Affected Images:** 10-8-34-216.sslip.io:5000/openshift/release-images@sha256:9b20418e019c81adf3e553b37405d84a6ee1c62f90902a355195a454df7bbaef (4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-032308); registry.redhat.io/openshift4/ose-must-gather:latest; ci-telco-runner (ansible/kcli cluster setup). PTP operator images were not exercised — telco5g-ptp-tests step did not execute.

**Is it a flake?** yes — prior 3 builds (2045291541634748416, 2042754953465827328, 2040218314893955072) passed cluster-setup on the same periodic job; only this run failed. Likely bare-metal lab or nightly-payload install instability, not a ptp-operator regression.

**Issue Class:** infra_other

**Related Source Files:**
- https://steps.ci.openshift.org/reference/telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup (cluster setup CI step)
- https://steps.ci.openshift.org/job?org=openshift&repo=release&branch=main&test=e2e-telco5g-ptp&variant=nightly-4.12 (job definition)
- https://prow.ci.openshift.org/view/gs/test-platform-results/logs/periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.12-e2e-telco5g-ptp/2063048605291253760 (failed

ba2e3f4453f86d85.root_cause: The telco5g 4.13 CI job runs **main-branch conformance tests** (`TEST_BRANCH=main`) against **release-4.13 component images** (`PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.13`, `USE_UPSTREAM=false`). The four failing tests were added on main in March–April 2026 and require PTP Operator / linuxptp-daemon / cloud
ba2e3f4453f86d85.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The telco5g 4.13 CI job runs **main-branch conformance tests** (`TEST_BRANCH=main`) against **release-4.13 component images** (`PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.13`, `USE_UPSTREAM=false`). The four failing tests were added on main in March–April 2026 and require PTP Operator / linuxptp-daemon / cloud-event-proxy behavior that is **not present on release-4.13** (confirmed: commits `0096e6b`, `da824bf`, `6d2f1dd`, `8b697a3` are not ancestors of `release-4.13`).

Two distinct failure mechanisms:

1. **ClockSync “Verify clockClass when locking PTP source” (bc + dualnicbc):** Tests call `EnableGMCapableInPlace()` to patch the PtpConfig CR (`gmCapable 0` → `gmCapable 1`), then poll `/var/run/ptp4l.0.config` for `gmCapable 1` via `WaitForConfigContent()`. On the release-4.13 stack the runtime config never updates within 120s, so the poll times out. Pod logs show clock-class events stuck at value `6` and no config-reload activity during the test window.

2. **Outage recovery “BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage” (bc + dualnicbc):** After `TurnAllPortsDown()`, the test expects the Prometheus clock-class metric to leave Locked (6). With `gmCapable 0` in the BC test config and release-4.13 T-BC clock-class handling, the metric stays at 6. Additionally, `TurnAllPortsDown()`/`TurnAllPortsUp()` did **not** disable NetworkManager (unlike `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty()`, fixed in PR #215), so RHCOS NetworkManager can auto-recover admin-down interfaces and keep the BC locked to its upstream GM.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** The tests that fail on this job were introduced by:
- https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/pull/197 (merged https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/0096e6bacb27afbd208d2101edf41c288c03bcb2) — clockClass locking tests
- https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/pull/185 (merged https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/da824bf082cd75c19fa56a4133902824eb5c2e5e) — BC outage clock-class recovery t

fa2536d0f721ff9b.root_cause: The telco5g-ptp CI job runs **main-branch conformance tests** against **release-4.14** container images (`ptp-operator:4.14`, `linuxptp-daemon:4.14`, `cloud-event-proxy:4.14`) on OCP 4.14.0. Three clock-class tests failed because they assert against the Prometheus metric `openshift_ptp_clock_class`,
fa2536d0f721ff9b.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The telco5g-ptp CI job runs **main-branch conformance tests** against **release-4.14** container images (`ptp-operator:4.14`, `linuxptp-daemon:4.14`, `cloud-event-proxy:4.14`) on OCP 4.14.0. Three clock-class tests failed because they assert against the Prometheus metric `openshift_ptp_clock_class`, which on OCP 4.14 does **not** update when ptp4l transitions clock class after upstream sync loss.

CI logs prove ptp4l itself is working: in the locking test, after bringing down slave interface `ens2f3`, **PMC immediately reports clock class 248** while the node-level metric **stays at 6** for the full 5-minute timeout:

```
PMC clock class for /var/run/ptp4l.0.config: 248 (expected 248)
Node-level clock class metric: 6 (expected 248)
```

On OCP 4.14, `openshift_ptp_clock_class` is maintained by **cloud-event-proxy** parsing `CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE` lines from ptp4l logs. When a BC loses sync (with `gmCapable=1`), ptp4l updates its internal parent dataset (visible via PMC) but does not emit the log line cloud-event-proxy needs to refresh the metric. The outage-recovery tests hit the same stale-metric problem; they also use `gmCapable=0` test configs where ptp4l will not degrade clock class on sync loss without an in-place `gmCapable=1` edit.

Additionally, `TurnAllPortsDown` (used by outage tests) did not disable NetworkManager management, unlike `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty` fixed in PR #215 — allowing potential interface auto-recovery during outage simulation.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/210 (commit https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/8b697a3517bc2e24dde547624a96b6d78c4d7a56) — CNF-22878 added node-level `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric verification for OCP < 4.18. That exposed the pre-existing 4.14 cloud-event-proxy limitation where the metric is set once at lock (class 6) but never updated on transition. The outage-recovery test itself was introduced in https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/1

922b3ccbfe80cd29.root_cause: Three PTP conformance tests failed in the BC and dualnicbc test modes on OCP 4.15. All failures involve clock-class verification during simulated upstream link loss:
922b3ccbfe80cd29.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** Three PTP conformance tests failed in the BC and dualnicbc test modes on OCP 4.15. All failures involve clock-class verification during simulated upstream link loss:

1. **Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on single NIC boundary clock** (bc-serial): PMC correctly reported clockClass 248 after bringing ens2f3 down, but the node-level `openshift_ptp_clock_class` Prometheus metric stayed at 6 for 300s. On OCP < 4.18, cloud-event-proxy exposes only a node-level clock_class metric (no per-config label) that tracks the main clock via `GetMainClockName()`. Per-config freerun transitions are visible via PMC but not reflected in that aggregate metric. PR #210 added node-level metric assertions for OCP < 4.18 single-NIC BC, which is incorrect for freerun (248) checks.

2. **BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage** (bc-serial and dualnicbc-serial): After `TurnAllPortsDown()`, clock class never degraded from Locked (6). NetworkManager on RHCOS auto-recovers admin-down interfaces within ~4–7s, restoring sync before the test could observe degradation. PR #215 fixed this for `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty()` via `nmcli managed=no`, but `TurnAllPortsDown()`/`TurnAllPortsUp()` used by the recovery test were never updated.

**Breaking PR/Commit:**
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/210 (commit https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/8b697a3517bc2e24dde547624a96b6d78c4d7a56) — introduced incorrect node-level metric assertion for freerun on OCP < 4.18
- https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/185 (commit https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/da824bf082cd75c19fa56a4133902824eb5c2e5e) — added recovery test using `TurnAllPortsDown` without NM guard (incomplete fix in https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/215)

**Affected Images:**
- `openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.15` (operator under test)
- linuxptp-daemon image (`DAEMON_IMAGE` in CI — hosts ptp4l/phc2sys and metrics endpoint)
- cloud-event-proxy sidecar

7f2e9aff26f320c2.root_cause: The `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet was never created because the PTP operator never started. The telco5g-ptp CI job builds the operator image in-cluster via a `podman` pod, but that build failed:
7f2e9aff26f320c2.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet was never created because the PTP operator never started. The telco5g-ptp CI job builds the operator image in-cluster via a `podman` pod, but that build failed:

```
Error: creating build container: ... registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18: manifest unknown
make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125
Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.18: image not known
```

The podman pod script runs `set +xe` before registry login and only re-enables `set -x` (not `set -e`), so the build failure is ignored and the pod exits 0. CI then runs `make deploy` with the missing image. Gather artifacts confirm `ptp-operator-7dff4cdf56-k44jf` is stuck in `ImagePullBackOff` ("trying and failing to pull image"). With no running operator, the `PtpOperatorConfig` reconciler never creates the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet, and the test times out after 400s polling `kubectl rollout status daemonset linuxptp-daemon -n openshift-ptp`.

This is a deployment/bootstrap failure, not a PTP clock-sync or ptp4l offset issue.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not a recent ptp-operator functional regression. The `release-4.18` Dockerfile has referenced the internal CI builder image since branch creation: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/00537cc78aeb322a2719eff140f6faf769f09e4b. The telco CI script (in openshift/release) builds with `make docker-build` for OCP 4.12–4.18, which requires pulling `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18` from the bare-metal telco lab — that registry is not reachable there. Contributing factor: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/670 (hermetic build fix on release-4.18, merged 2026-03-17) did not change this, but the CI script error-handling gap allows silent continuation after build failure.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.1

a8893477c251bcee.root_cause: Four PTP serial conformance tests failed on OCP 4.17, all involving T-BC clock-class behavior after simulated upstream link loss. Full JUnit from GCS shows:
a8893477c251bcee.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** Four PTP serial conformance tests failed on OCP 4.17, all involving T-BC clock-class behavior after simulated upstream link loss. Full JUnit from GCS shows:

1. `[bc-serial] Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on single NIC boundary clock` — node-level `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric stayed at **6** (Locked) for 5 minutes; test expected **248** (Freerun) at `ptptesthelper.go:754`.
2. `[bc-serial]`, `[dualnicbc-serial]`, `[dualnicbcha-serial] BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage` — clock class never degraded from **6** after slave interfaces were brought down (`ptp.go:2155`).

Two separate test-framework gaps explain this:

- **Outage recovery (3 failures):** `TurnAllPortsDown`/`TurnAllPortsUp` use `ip link set down/up` without disabling NetworkManager. PR #215 fixed this for `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty` via `nmcli device set <iface> managed no`, but the outage test added in PR #185 still uses `TurnAllPortsDown`, so NetworkManager auto-recovers interfaces within ~4–7 seconds. Port roles briefly reach FAULTY, but the link comes back up and clock class stays at 6.
- **Clock-class locking (1 failure):** On OCP 4.17, `openshift_ptp_clock_class` is a node-level gauge without a `config` label (per-config labels arrive in 4.18). CNF-22878 added node-level metric verification for single-NIC BC, which works for the initial locked state (6) but does not reliably track BC transitions to freerun (248). PMC reports 248 correctly; the Prometheus metric does not update.

**Breaking PR/Commit:**
- Outage test gap: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/185 (added outage recovery test using `TurnAllPortsDown` without NM handling)
- Incomplete NM fix: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/6d2f1ddce64b84110bcf44abcb9b9b7d292f93c0 (PR https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/pull/215 — only applied NM workaround to `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty`, not `TurnAllPortsDown`)
- Metrics regression on 4.17: https://git

fb5b5817f5cf323c.root_cause: The `telco5g-ptp-tests` step never reached the PTP ginkgo test suite. After successfully building and deploying `ptp-operator` from `release-4.20`, the job hung on `kubectl rollout status daemonset linuxptp-daemon -n openshift-ptp`, waiting for the operator-created DaemonSet to finish rolling out (i
fb5b5817f5cf323c.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The `telco5g-ptp-tests` step never reached the PTP ginkgo test suite. After successfully building and deploying `ptp-operator` from `release-4.20`, the job hung on `kubectl rollout status daemonset linuxptp-daemon -n openshift-ptp`, waiting for the operator-created DaemonSet to finish rolling out (it reached 5/6 pods available, then regressed to 1/6 during a rolling update). At ~07:33 UTC the API watch stream dropped (`http2: client connection lost`), and by ~07:45 UTC all API calls returned `Unauthorized` / `the server has asked for the client to provide credentials`. The step ran until the 3h45m Prow timeout at 08:56 UTC (exit code 127 from forced termination). Post-step gather steps (`gather-must-gather`, `gather-extra`, `gather-audit-logs`) failed as cascading symptoms because the cluster API was completely unreachable. No individual PTP test case failed — tests were never executed.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not identifiable. No evidence of a `ptp-operator` code regression. The job tests the `release-4.20` branch (https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/tree/release-4.20) on bare-metal cluster `ptpcimno` (RDU2 telco5g lab). The three most recent prior runs of this same job all passed (build IDs `2027247662239059968`, `2026523068867809280`, `2025798196667617280`), indicating an isolated infrastructure failure rather than a code change.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.20` — built and deployed successfully in CI
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.20` — referenced by operator, not built in this downstream run
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.20` — referenced, not built in this downstream run
- Cluster under test: OpenShift `4.21.0-0.nightly-2026-06-08-132701` on 6-node bare-metal (`ptpcimno` plan)
- CI runner: `ci/telco-runner:latest`, release payload `registry.ci.openshift.org/o

62e7bf93d819ab57.root_cause: The nightly `e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream` job failed with **6 test errors** across multiple PTP modes on a shared cluster. Failures fall into three categories:
62e7bf93d819ab57.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The nightly `e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream` job failed with **6 test errors** across multiple PTP modes on a shared cluster. Failures fall into three categories:

1. **Dual-follower PTP state transition failure (primary):** `[dualfollower-serial] Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down` timed out after 10 minutes waiting for **HOLDOVER** on `ens7f0`; metrics reported **FREERUN** instead (`ptptesthelper.go:93`). The test deletes the grandmaster PtpConfig and expects holdover→freerun transitions; the daemon did not enter holdover as expected.

2. **Test regression — duplicate temp profile delete:** `[dualfollower-serial] Can provide a profile with higher priority` failed at `ptp.go:991` with `ptpconfigs.ptp.openshift.io "temp" not found`. PR #236 removed pod restart in favor of configmap hot-reload but left both a `DeferCleanup` delete and an explicit delete step; the temp config was already gone when the explicit delete ran.

3. **Cascade failures from unreleased cluster state:** The dual-follower test deletes the GM and only recreates it at the end. When it fails mid-test, the GM stays deleted, breaking later modes on the same cluster:
   - `[dualnicbcha-serial] Reboot discovery` — `no solution found for Dual NIC BC configuration in Local GM mode` (`ptp.go:369`)
   - `[oc-serial] Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down` — `no linuxptp-daemon pod found matching ptpconfig test-grandmaster` (`ptp.go:521`)

4. **Independent T-GM / infra issues (ran first, not caused by cascade):**
   - `[tgm-serial] WPC GM state based on metrics` — timed out waiting for `openshift_ptp_frequency_status`; GM was in FREERUN/LISTENING, `cne_events_received{status="failed"}` on all event types, linuxptp-daemon pods restarted mid-test
   - `[tgm-parallel] PTP CPU Utilization` — `Failed to get a valid response from prometheus after retries` (`parallel/ptp.go:267`)

**Breaking PR/Commit:** https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/pull/236 (

e4e8d95e6f629b7f.root_cause: The nightly `e2e-telco5g-ptp` job failed in **DualNICBCHA** mode only. The test `[dualnicbcha-serial] PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master` timed out after 600s while validating the secondary boundary-clock PtpConfig `test-bc-master2` on node `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com`. The primary B
e4e8d95e6f629b7f.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The nightly `e2e-telco5g-ptp` job failed in **DualNICBCHA** mode only. The test `[dualnicbcha-serial] PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master` timed out after 600s while validating the secondary boundary-clock PtpConfig `test-bc-master2` on node `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com`. The primary BC (`test-bc-master1`, interface `ens2f1`) synced successfully, but the secondary slave interface `ens4f2` was reported as **FAULTY** in the `openshift_ptp_interface_role` Prometheus metric for the entire 10-minute `Eventually` window. Daemon logs show `ens4f2` actually reached **SLAVE** at 15:55:38 UTC (`ptp4l.1.config`), with no corresponding `SLAVE → FAULTY` transition in ptp4l logs until 16:09 (after the test ended). This is a **metrics/state desync** after linuxptp-daemon reconfiguration: immediately before the failure, the underscore HA profile test (15:54:21) and `EnableEventPublisher` toggle (15:54:22) triggered daemon/sidecar restarts (15:54:37, 15:55:33), leaving stale `interface_role=FAULTY` metrics even though ptp4l had re-synced.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** No single definitive regression commit identified. The failure pattern matches a known CI flake class addressed in https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/3586177d40d8095340f46a62adf94d13d5ffabf6 (pod/daemon restarts causing port-state confusion in netdevsim CI). The immediate trigger was daemon churn from tests running just before ClockSync in the same suite — notably the underscore HA profile test (https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L273) and the `EnableEventPublisher` toggle test (https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L312) — not a new product-code regression on `release-5.0`.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.../openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0` (built from `release-5.0`)
- `image-registry.../openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0`
- `image-registry.../openshift-ptp/cloud-event

379e371690d1d4ed.root_cause: The `telco5g-ptp-tests` step failed because the first test mode (`PTP_TEST_MODE=tgm`) exited with status 2 (`temp_status_tgm=2`), which caused the overall job to fail. In TGM mode, the PTP operator was not present in `openshift-ptp` when tests started (~12:41 UTC): `deployments.apps/ptp-operator` an
379e371690d1d4ed.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The `telco5g-ptp-tests` step failed because the first test mode (`PTP_TEST_MODE=tgm`) exited with status 2 (`temp_status_tgm=2`), which caused the overall job to fail. In TGM mode, the PTP operator was not present in `openshift-ptp` when tests started (~12:41 UTC): `deployments.apps/ptp-operator` and `ptpoperatorconfigs.ptp.openshift.io/default` returned NotFound. That led to 6 serial failures and 1 parallel failure. A `make deploy` from `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator` main had succeeded minutes earlier (deployment created, `default` PtpOperatorConfig patched, daemonset rolled out), but by test time those resources were gone. `linuxptp-daemon` pods were then stuck in `ContainerCreating`/`Pending` for 300+ seconds, causing topology discovery to fail (`Status=discoveryFailure`). A Prometheus query failure in the soak test added another failure. This rehearsal run was triggered by openshift/release PR 80182, which changed CI to use fork branch `yaronh12/ptp-operator:ts2phc-gm-deletion-tests-coverage` for conformance tests and set `SKIP_OCP_INSTALL=true` (skipping NTP disable and full cluster setup).

**Breaking PR/Commit:** https://github.com/openshift/release/pull/80182 — a "TEST ONLY - DO NOT MERGE" rehearsal PR that modified:
- `ci-operator/step-registry/telco5g/ptp/tests/telco5g-ptp-tests-commands.sh` (conformance tests cloned from `yaronh12/ptp-operator` branch `ts2phc-gm-deletion-tests-coverage` instead of upstream `k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator`)
- `ci-operator/step-registry/telco5g/ptp-cluster-setup/telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup-commands.sh` (added `SKIP_OCP_INSTALL="true"`)

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.22`
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22`
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.22`

**Is it a flake?** yes — The failure pattern is timing/environment dependent. Operator
